mission.title: argo: Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy
mission.description: Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy. Requested by 95+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Check current Namespace configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get namespace -A
kubectl describe namespace <name> -n argo
```
Adding support for some mechanism to allow ArgoCD to delete the namespace of its underlying app. This could be limited to apps that were created with a `CreateNamespace` SyncPolicy set.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy
steps.description:
- After this PR is merged, we can specify `DeleteNamespace` syncOption, which enables a feature to delete an auto-created namespace.
- We can delete a namespace only when:
  - `CreateNamespace=true` and `DeleteNamespace=true`
  - The target namespace is tracked by an application. 
    - e.g.) The
```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: guestbook
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc/
    namespace: test
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/argoproj/argocd-example-apps.git
    path: guestbook
    targetRevision: HEAD
  syncPolicy:
    syncOptions:
    - CreateNamespace=true
    - DeleteNamespace=true
    managedNamespaceMetadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: guestbook
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for DeleteNamespace SyncPolicy" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
- After this PR is merged, we can specify `DeleteNamespace` syncOption, which enables a feature to delete an auto-created namespace.
- We can delete a namespace only when:
  - `CreateNamespace=true` and `DeleteNamespace=true`
  - The target namespace is tracked by an application. 
    - e.g.) The namespace must have a label, `app.kubernetes.io/instance=${applicationName}`

kind: Application
metadata:
  name: guestbook
  namespace: argocd
spec:

  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc/
    namespace: test
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/argoproj/argocd-example-apps.git
    path: guestbook
    targetRevision: HEAD
  syncPolicy:
    syncOptions:
    - CreateNamespace=true
    - DeleteNamespace=true
    managedNamespaceMetadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: guestbook

  syncPolicy:
    syncOptions:
    - CreateNamespace=true
    - DeleteNamespace=true
    managedNamespaceMetadata:

        app.kubernetes.io/instance: guestbook

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
